data.content:
### About repository invitations

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/collaborators/invitations.md

This section provides an overview of the repository invitations API. It explains how to view and manage invitations to collaborate on a repository. Invited users can accept or decline these invitations. It also clarifies the difference between adding a collaborator and inviting one, and details the required OAuth scopes.

```APIDOC
## About repository invitations

You can view and manage invitations to collaborate on a repository. The invited users (or external services on behalf of invited users) can choose to accept or decline the invitations.

To add a user as a collaborator, use the Collaborators endpoints instead. For more information, see [AUTOTITLE](/rest/collaborators/collaborators#add-a-repository-collaborator).

Note that the `repo:invite` [OAuth scope](/apps/oauth-apps/building-oauth-apps/scopes-for-oauth-apps) grants targeted access to invitations **without** also granting access to repository code, while the `repo` scope grants permission to code as well as invitations.
```

--------------------------------

### Search within a user's repositories

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/search-github/github-code-search/understanding-github-code-search-syntax.md

Employ the `user:` qualifier to search for files exclusively within the repositories owned by a particular personal account. This is ideal for inspecting a specific developer's contributions.

```text
user:octocat
```

--------------------------------

### User Interactions API

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/interactions/user.md

Manage interaction limits for users on GitHub. This API allows you to control the behavior of different user types (existing, contributor, collaborator) in your public repositories.

```APIDOC
## About User Interactions

You can use the REST API to temporarily restrict which type of user can comment, open issues, or create pull requests on your public repositories. This documentation outlines the general capabilities for managing user interaction limits.

### Purpose
To control the interaction levels of different user types (existing, contributor, collaborator) on public repositories.

### Scope
This API manages user-level interaction limits, which override repository-level limits for repositories owned by the user.

### Related Endpoints
For repository-specific interaction limits, refer to the [Repository Interactions API](/rest/interactions/repos).

### User Types
*   **Existing User:** Users who have already committed to your repository.
*   **Contributor User:** Users who have already contributed to your repository.
*   **Collaborator User:** Users who have already collaborated on your repository.
```

--------------------------------

### View Remote Repository URL for Username

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/account-and-profile/how-tos/email-preferences/remembering-your-github-username-or-email.md

This command displays the URLs for the remote repositories. By examining the fetch and push URLs, you can identify your GitHub username, which appears after the domain name and before the repository name. This method is effective for personal repositories or forks.

```shell
cd YOUR-REPOSITORY
git remote -v
```

--------------------------------

### PUT /orgs/{org}/teams/{team_slug}/repos/{owner}/{repo}

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/data/reusables/organizations/team-api.md

Add or update a team's repository permission. You can add a repository to a team or update the team's permission level for an existing repository.

```APIDOC
## PUT /orgs/{org}/teams/{team_slug}/repos/{owner}/{repo}

### Description
Adds a repository to a team or updates the team's permission for a repository.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
`/orgs/{org}/teams/{team_slug}/repos/{owner}/{repo}`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **org** (string) - Required - The organization's name.
- **team_slug** (string) - Required - The URL-safe version of the team's name.
- **owner** (string) - Required - The owner of the repository (organization or user).
- **repo** (string) - Required - The name of the repository.

#### Request Body
- **permission** (string) - Required - The permission level to add or update. Accepted values are `pull` (Read) and `push` (Write).

### Request Example
```json
{
  "example": "PUT /orgs/my-org/teams/my-team/repos/my-org/my-repo"
}
```
```json
{
  "example": "{
    \"permission\": \"push\"
  }"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (204)
No content. Indicates the operation was successful.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "example": ""
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Export Repository List as JSON

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/code-security/codeql-for-vs-code/getting-started-with-codeql-for-vs-code/running-codeql-queries-at-scale-with-multi-repository-variant-analysis.md

This JSON snippet represents a custom list of repositories generated for variant analysis. It includes a list name and an array of repository names. This format is used for exporting and importing repository lists, facilitating collaboration and re-analysis.

```json
{
    "name": "new-repo-list",
    "repositories": [
        "my-organization/my-repository"
    ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Git user information (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/migrations/importing-source-code/using-the-command-line-to-import-source-code/importing-a-team-foundation-version-control-repository.md

This command configures your global Git user name and email address. These credentials will be associated with your commits. Replace 'NAME' and 'EMAIL' with your actual name and email address. This is a prerequisite for pushing to GitHub.

```shell
git config --global user.name "NAME"
git config --global user.email EMAIL
```

--------------------------------

### Set Repository Interaction Restrictions

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/interactions/repos.md

Temporarily restrict who can comment, open issues, or create pull requests in a public repository. This endpoint is equivalent to the {% data variables.product.repo-interactions-settings-page %} page.

```APIDOC
## PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits

### Description

Sets or updates the interaction restriction settings for a specific repository.

### Method

PUT

### Endpoint

`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits`

### Parameters

#### Path Parameters

- **owner** (string) - Required - The account owner of the repository.
- **repo** (string) - Required - The name of the repository.

#### Request Body

- **limit** (string) - Required - The interaction limit type ('one_day', 'one_week', 'one_month', 'three_months', 'six_months').

### Request Example

```json
{
  "example": "PUT /repos/octocat/Spoon-Knife/interaction-limits"
}
```

```json
{
  "example": {
    "limit": "three_months"
  }
}
```

### Response

#### Success Response (200)

- **limit** (string) - The interaction limit type (e.g., 'one_day', 'one_week', 'one_month', 'three_months', 'six_months').
- **origin** (string) - The source of the interaction limit (e.g., 'user', 'org', 'repository').
- **created_at** (string) - The timestamp when the interaction limit was created.
- **updated_at** (string) - The timestamp when the interaction limit was last updated.

#### Response Example

```json
{
  "example": {
    "limit": "three_months",
    "origin": "repository",
    "created_at": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2023-10-27T11:30:00Z"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Edit GPG Key to Add User ID

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/authentication/managing-commit-signature-verification/associating-an-email-with-your-gpg-key.md

This command opens an existing GPG key for editing. You will be prompted to add user ID details, including your real name, email address, and comments. Ensure the email address matches your verified GitHub committer identity.

```shell
gpg --edit-key GPG_KEY_ID
gpg> adduid
Real Name: YOUR_NAME
Email address: "your_email@example.com"
Comment: GITHUB_KEY
Change (N)ame, (C)omment, (E)mail or (O)kay/(Q)uit?
O
gpg> save
```

--------------------------------

### Reference Local Action from Private Repository

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/workflow-syntax.md

This example shows how to use a GitHub Action defined in a private repository. It requires checking out the repository using a personal access token or GitHub App and then referencing the action locally. The `token` input requires a secret containing the access token.

```yaml
jobs:
  my_first_job:
    steps:
      - name: Check out repository
        uses: {% data reusables.actions.action-checkout %}
        with:
          repository: octocat/my-private-repo
          ref: v1.0
          token: {% raw %}${{ secrets.PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}{% endraw %}
          path: ./.github/actions/my-private-repo
      - name: Run my action
        uses: ./.github/actions/my-private-repo/my-action
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Repository with PAT on Command Line

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens.md

This example demonstrates how to clone a repository using a Personal Access Token (PAT) on the command line. When prompted for a password, enter your PAT. The username is required but not used for authentication; the PAT is the sole authentication credential.

```shell
$ git clone https://github.com/USERNAME/REPO.git
Username: YOUR-USERNAME
Password: YOUR-PERSONAL-ACCESS-TOKEN
```

--------------------------------

### GraphQL Interface for RepositoryDiscussionAuthor

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/graphql/guides/using-the-graphql-api-for-discussions.md

Defines an interface for entities that can author discussions in a repository, such as Users and Organizations. It includes a field `repositoryDiscussions` to fetch discussions authored by the entity, with options for pagination, filtering by answer status, and ordering.

```graphql
"""
Represents an author of discussions in repositories.
"""
interface RepositoryDiscussionAuthor {
  """
  Discussions this user has started.
  """
  repositoryDiscussions(
    """
    Returns the elements in the list that come after the specified cursor.
    """
    after: String

    """
    Filter discussions to only those that have been answered or not. Defaults to
    including both answered and unanswered discussions.
    """
    answered: Boolean = null

    """
    Returns the elements in the list that come before the specified cursor.
    """
    before: String

    """
    Returns the first _n_ elements from the list.
    """
    first: Int

    """
    Returns the last _n_ elements from the list.
    """
    last: Int

    """
    Ordering options for discussions returned from the connection.
    """
    orderBy: DiscussionOrder = {field: CREATED_AT, direction: DESC}

    """
    Filter discussions to only those in a specific repository.
    """
    repositoryId: ID
  ): DiscussionConnection!
}

```

--------------------------------

### Create a .gitignore file

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/get-started/git-basics/ignoring-files.md

This command creates a `.gitignore` file in the root directory of your Git repository. This file tells Git which files and directories to ignore when you make a commit. Changes to this file are typically committed to the repository to share ignore rules with collaborators.

```shell
touch .gitignore
```

--------------------------------

### Remove Repository Interaction Restrictions

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/interactions/repos.md

Stop restricting who can comment, open issues, or create pull requests in a public repository. This endpoint is equivalent to the {% data variables.product.repo-interactions-settings-page %} page.

```APIDOC
## DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits

### Description

Removes all interaction restrictions for a specific repository.

### Method

DELETE

### Endpoint

`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/interaction-limits`

### Parameters

#### Path Parameters

- **owner** (string) - Required - The account owner of the repository.
- **repo** (string) - Required - The name of the repository.

### Request Example

```json
{
  "example": "DELETE /repos/octocat/Spoon-Knife/interaction-limits"
}
```

### Response

#### Success Response (204)

- No content is returned on successful deletion.

#### Response Example

```json
{
  "example": ""
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Build Production Docker Image Locally

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/src/deployments/production/README.md

Builds the production Docker image locally using Docker BuildKit to support secret mounting for private repository cloning. Requires a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) with 'contents: read' access to specified repositories.

```bash
docker build -t docs:latest . --secret id=DOCS_BOT_PAT_BASE,src=<(echo "<your GH PAT value>")
```

--------------------------------

### Add a repository to a user secret

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/codespaces/secrets.md

Adds a repository to a user secret. This secret is made available to the codespace at runtime.

```APIDOC
## PUT /user/codespaces/secrets/{secret_name}/repositories/{repository_id}

### Description
Adds a repository to a user secret. This secret is made available to the codespace at runtime.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
/user/codespaces/secrets/{secret_name}/repositories/{repository_id}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **secret_name** (string) - Required - The name of the secret.
- **repository_id** (integer) - Required - The ID of the repository to add.

### Response
#### Success Response (204)
No content is returned upon successful addition.

#### Error Response (422)
- **message** (string) - The error message.
- **errors** (array) - An array of error objects.
  - **resource** (string) - The resource that caused the error.
  - **field** (string) - The field that caused the error.
  - **code** (string) - The error code.

#### Error Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Validation failed, and the endpoint could not be modernized.",
  "errors": [
    {
      "resource": "SecretRepository",
      "field": "repository_id",
      "code": "invalid"
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### REST API endpoints for collaborators

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/rest/projects-classic/collaborators.md

Endpoints for managing collaborators on a Project, including adding, removing, and listing collaborators.

```APIDOC
## GET /projects/{project_id}/collaborators

### Description
Lists the collaborators for a project.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/projects/{project_id}/collaborators

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **project_id** (integer) - Required - The unique identifier of the project.

### Request Example
(No request body for GET requests)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **collaborators** (array) - A list of collaborator objects.
  - **login** (string) - The username of the collaborator.
  - **id** (integer) - The user ID of the collaborator.
  - **node_id** (string) - The GraphQL node ID of the collaborator.
  - **url** (string) - The URL to the collaborator's profile.
  - **html_url** (string) - The URL to the collaborator's HTML profile.
  - **type** (string) - The type of collaborator (e.g., 'user').
  - **permissions** (object) - The permissions the collaborator has on the project.
    - **read** (boolean) - Whether the collaborator can read the project.
    - **write** (boolean) - Whether the collaborator can write to the project.
    - **admin** (boolean) - Whether the collaborator has admin permissions on the project.

#### Response Example
{
  "collaborators": [
    {
      "login": "octocat",
      "id": 1,
      "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjE=",
      "url": "https://api.github.com/users/octocat",
      "html_url": "https://github.com/octocat",
      "type": "user",
      "permissions": {
        "read": true,
        "write": true,
        "admin": true
      }
    }
  ]
}

## PUT /projects/{project_id}/collaborators/{username}

### Description
Adds a collaborator to a project.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
/projects/{project_id}/collaborators/{username}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **project_id** (integer) - Required - The unique identifier of the project.
- **username** (string) - Required - The username of the collaborator to add.

#### Request Body
- **permission** (string) - Optional - The permission level to grant to the collaborator. Can be one of 'read', 'write', or 'admin'. Defaults to 'write'.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "permission": "admin"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (204)
No content.

#### Response Example
(No response body for 204 No Content)

## DELETE /projects/{project_id}/collaborators/{username}

### Description
Removes a collaborator from a project.

### Method
DELETE

### Endpoint
/projects/{project_id}/collaborators/{username}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **project_id** (integer) - Required - The unique identifier of the project.
- **username** (string) - Required - The username of the collaborator to remove.

### Request Example
(No request body for DELETE requests)

### Response
#### Success Response (204)
No content.

#### Response Example
(No response body for 204 No Content)
```

--------------------------------

### CLI: Set Environment Variables for Migration

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/migrations/ado/migrating-repositories-from-azure-devops-to-github-enterprise-cloud.md

Guides on setting environment variables for GitHub Personal Access Tokens (PATs) required for migration.

```APIDOC
## CLI Commands

### Set Environment Variables

Before running migration commands, set the following environment variables:

- **`GH_PAT`**: Your GitHub Personal Access Token for the destination organization.
- **`ADO_PAT`**: Your Azure DevOps Personal Access Token for the source organization.

**For Terminal (Linux/macOS):**

```bash
export GH_PAT="YOUR_GITHUB_PAT"
export ADO_PAT="YOUR_AZURE_DEVOPS_PAT"
```

**For PowerShell (Windows):**

```powershell
$env:GH_PAT="YOUR_GITHUB_PAT"
$env:ADO_PAT="YOUR_AZURE_DEVOPS_PAT"
```

*Ensure you replace `YOUR_GITHUB_PAT` and `YOUR_AZURE_DEVOPS_PAT` with your actual tokens.*

**Note:** For Azure DevOps PATs, ensure the required scopes are granted. Refer to the documentation for specific scope requirements.
```

--------------------------------

### Download migration log using ADO2GH CLI

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/migrations/using-github-enterprise-importer/completing-your-migration-with-github-enterprise-importer/accessing-your-migration-logs-for-github-enterprise-importer.md

This command downloads the latest migration log for an individual repository when the migration source is Azure DevOps. It requires specifying the target organization, target repository, and a filename for the log. Ensure you have the necessary personal access token scopes and API URLs configured.

```shell
gh ado2gh download-logs --github-target-org DESTINATION --target-repo REPOSITORY --migration-log-file FILENAME
```

--------------------------------

### Example .gitattributes file for repository line ending configuration

Source: https://github.com/github/docs/blob/main/content/get-started/git-basics/configuring-git-to-handle-line-endings.md

An example `.gitattributes` file that can be used to manage line endings in a specific Git repository. This file overrides global `core.autocrlf` settings and ensures consistent line ending behavior for all collaborators. It defines rules for text files, binary files, and specific file types.

```text
# Set the default behavior, in case people don't have core.autocrlf set.
* text=auto

# Explicitly declare text files you want to always be normalized and converted
# to native line endings on checkout.
*.c text
*.h text

# Declare files that will always have CRLF line endings on checkout.
*.sln text eol=crlf

# Denote all files that are truly binary and should not be modified.
*.png binary
*.jpg binary
```
